name: North African Spiced Shepherd's Pie
description: A harissa-spiced lentil and mushroom filling with a golden olive oil mash — warming, hearty, and even better the next day.

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: to taste; source listed as combined 'salt and black pepper'

ingredients.name: black pepper
ingredients.notes: to taste; source listed as combined 'salt and black pepper'

steps.title: Cook the lentils
steps.content: Rinse {0010} and simmer in plenty of water for 20–25 minutes until just tender but still holding their shape. Drain and set aside.

steps.title: Build the base
steps.content: Heat {0001} in a large pan over medium heat. Add {0006} and let them sizzle for 30 seconds, then add {0002}, {0003} and {0004}. Cook for 8–10 minutes until softened.

steps.title: Add garlic and spices
steps.content: Add {0005}, {0007} and {0008}. Stir well and cook for 2 minutes until fragrant.

steps.title: Add mushrooms
steps.content: Add {0009} and cook for 5 minutes until they've released their moisture and started to colour.

steps.title: Build the sauce
steps.content: Add {0012}, {0013} and {0014}. Stir to combine, then add the cooked lentils and {0011}. Simmer on a low heat for 15–20 minutes until thick and rich. It should not be sloppy — let it reduce properly. Finish with {0015} and season well with {0018} and {0019}.

steps.title: Make the mash
steps.content: While the filling simmers, boil {0016} in salted water until tender, around 20 minutes. Drain thoroughly, then mash with {0017} and a splash of the cooking water if needed. Season generously with {0018} and {0019}. You want it smooth and spreadable but not too loose.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.content: Preheat oven to 200°C. Spoon the filling into a baking dish. Spread the mash over the top evenly, then rough up the surface with a fork to create ridges — these will catch and brown nicely. Drizzle a little extra olive oil over the top.

steps.title: Bake
steps.content: Bake for 25–30 minutes until the top is golden and the filling is bubbling at the edges. Rest for 5 minutes before serving.

notes: The filling genuinely improves overnight as the harissa and spices develop. If reheating the next day, add a splash of water or stock before putting it back in the oven covered with foil, then uncover for the last 10 minutes to re-crisp the mash. You can add a little extra harissa on top of the mash before baking if you want more heat on the surface.

_fixture_notes.external_ref: Supplied slug 'north-african-spiced-shepherds-pie-stripped' used verbatim; not re-derived. Disambiguator suffix '-stripped' signals this is the plain-source-PDF version rather than the annotated worked-example fixture.
_fixture_notes.cook_time_minutes: No labelled timing fields in source. Stated step durations summed per the spec's stated-step-durations convention. Durations: Step 1 (lentils) 20 min lower bound; Step 2 (base) 8 min lower bound + 30 sec sizzle rounded to 8.5, taken as 8 for integer sum; Step 3 (spices) 2 min; Step 4 (mushrooms) 5 min; Steps 5 and 6 run concurrently (source states 'While the filling simmers, boil potatoes') — critical path is longer of 15 min (sauce simmer lower bound) and 20 min (potatoes) = 20 min; Step 8 (bake) 25 min lower bound. Post-cook rest (5 min) excluded per spec. Total: 20 + 8 + 2 + 5 + 20 + 25 = 80 min. cook_time_minutes set to 80.
_fixture_notes.prep_time_minutes: No labelled prep field and no stated prep duration separate from cooking steps. Left null.
_fixture_notes.salt_and_pepper: Source lists a single ingredient line '1 pinch salt and black pepper'. Combined-seasonings convention applied: split into two rows (0018 salt, 0019 black pepper), each with amount null and notes 'to taste'. The 'pinch' descriptor is a non-standard quantity — treated as functional per the non-standard quantities convention. Source's combined framing noted in both ingredient notes rows. Group assigned 'mash' as this is the only ingredient group the line appears under; salt is also referenced in step 5 (season the filling) and step 6 (salted boiling water) via {0018} and {0019} placeholders — this is a genuine dual-use case but the spec's duplicate-ingredient convention applies only when uses are in distinct groups. Both uses are within the same recipe scope and the salt/pepper rows are already group-labelled 'mash'; the filling seasoning reference in step 5 reuses those IDs. No additional rows created — flagged for operator review if shopping-list accuracy requires separate quantities.
_fixture_notes.miso_paste: Source lists 'miso paste' without specifying type. The worked example in the spec uses 'miso paste' identically. Carried across as written; soy restriction retained as miso paste universally contains soy.
_fixture_notes.dietary_restrictions: Soy: miso paste. No dairy, gluten, eggs, nuts, peanuts, shellfish, or sesame.
_fixture_notes.meal_types: Source has no explicit meal-type framing beyond a dinner-style dish. Assigned ['dinner'] only. The worked example in the spec includes 'lunch' and meal_formats: ['microwave'] based on a separate lunch options document not present in this source PDF — not carried across, as this conversion is from the source alone.
_fixture_notes.meal_formats: No meal-format framing in source. Left empty. (The worked example's 'microwave' classification derived from an external lunch options doc not present here.)
_fixture_notes.olive_oil_duplicate: Olive oil appears in two distinct uses: 3 tbsp for the filling (0001) and 3 tbsp for the mash (0017). Separate rows per duplicate-ingredient-across-groups convention.
_fixture_notes.white_beans: Source specifies '400g white beans (tinned, drained)'. Tin-weight convention: 400g used as amount (matching the tin weight). Drained weight not used.
_fixture_notes.no_derived_components: Batch mode — no derived components proposed. The lentil and bean filling is a strong derivation candidate for component_dinner/base, as in the spec's worked example. Flagged for operator review.
